title: ESP Outbound Bytes Spike versus Rolling Baseline

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic src_zone="ESP_INTERNAL" action=allowed earliest=-30d
| eval internal_dest=cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",dest) OR cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12",dest) OR cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16",dest)
| where internal_dest=0
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(bytes_out) as b_out by src, dest, _time
| eventstats median(b_out) as med by src, dest
| eval spike=if(b_out>med*8 AND med>0,1,0)
| where spike=1
| table _time, src, dest, b_out, med
value: We surface unusual outbound volume from ESP-internal segments that may indicate data exfiltration or misconfigured services.
implementation: (1) Tune multiplier and internal CIDRs. (2) Exclude CDN and update destinations via lookup. (3) Tag BES Cyber Assets on `src`. (4) Pair with PCAP workflow if available.
visualization: Time chart (bytes), Table (spikes), Single value (spike count)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1d | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
